free_text: Activation cross-sections of deuteron induced nuclear
reactions on rhenium up to 40 MeV
Natural Re in the form of Mo/Re alloy
respective weight percentages: 52.5/47.5
Stacks containing Mo/Re and other targets with Al and
Ti monitor foils were irradiated at 21 MeV (VUB) and
40 MeV (CYRIC). Irradiations were performed using
constant beam current of 160 nA for 60 min (VUB)
and 24 nA for 30 min (CYRIC).
The activity was measured without chemical separation
by high resolution gamma-ray spectroscopy.
The effective energy for each foil was determined
using the energy-range formula and tables of Andersen
and Ziegler, and corrected by comparing the
simultaneously re-measured cross sections of the
monitor reactions to the latest recommended values.
The activities were determined from the photo-peak
areas, taking into account corrections for decay
during irradiation, waiting and measuring time.
Decay data and the Q-values of the were taken from
NUDAT2 and from Q-value calculator of NNDC.
Acceptably good agreement was found
after moderate adjustment on the beam current (10%
compared to the Faraday cup measurement) and on the
incident beam energy (0.5 MeV).
Chapter 4
The uncertainty of the beam energy in the
middle of each irradiated foil was estimated
combining the uncertainties of the energy degradation
depending on the thickness of the target foils in the
stack and the energy straggling.
The uncertainty of the cross-sections were
estimated taking the independent relative errors of
linearly contributing components, number of the
bombarding particles, number of the target nuclei,
decay data, detector efficiency and peak area were
summed quadratically, and the square root of the sum
was taken.
The experimental data were compared to the
results of the theoretical model codes TALYS, EMPIRE-D
and ALICE-D.
The measured experimental data were compared to
those found in the literature.
Fitted excitation functions were used for
determination of integral physical yields.
